danger_signals.visual_form: Zombies, apparitions, hellhounds rendered with grotesque visual details
danger_signals.meaning: Active supernatural threat; area unsafe for prolonged habitation

danger_signals.visual_form: Zombie holding severed talking head and bloody knife (JP version); eyeball dangling from socket
danger_signals.meaning: Extreme danger; violent death has occurred; inhabited by corrupted magic users

danger_signals.visual_form: Pentagram on foyer rug (JP); changed to star in US version due to censorship
danger_signals.meaning: Demonic presence; sorcerous corruption active in location

danger_signals.visual_form: Green sky outside mansion; weird green clouds above stone maze; green lighting in laboratory
danger_signals.meaning: Magical corruption of natural world; reality distorted by sorcery

danger_signals.visual_form: Car bursting into flames at game start; deteriorating mansion structure
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural forces actively hostile; escape routes compromised

danger_signals.visual_form: Cross transformed to goblet (JP); crucifix changed to ornament (censored US version)
danger_signals.meaning: Holy ground corrupted; sorcerer's domain inverted from sacred space

danger_signals.visual_form: Cemetery maze; cross-shaped tombstones (JP); regular tombstones (US)
danger_signals.meaning: Death concentrated in specific zones; undead likely to manifest here

safety_signals.visual_form: Servant's bedroom with newer decor; game room with phonograph
safety_signals.meaning: Areas with active human history; some magical protections may remain

safety_signals.visual_form: Goblet of 'celestial water' on altar (US version); crucifix ornament
safety_signals.meaning: Holy objects offer protection or puzzle solutions; indicate consecrated ground

safety_signals.visual_form: Art gallery with paintings and sculptures; greenhouse
safety_signals.meaning: Spaces of human creativity and life; less likely to harbor active threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Lamps in fireplace that can be collected
safety_signals.meaning: Illumination grants agency; darkness is danger indicator

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pentagram (JP) / Star (US censored); early 20th century mansion architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dracan (frozen form in cavern; reawakening threat); corrupted apprentices rendered as zombies and apparitions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion structure; tower; underground cavern; occult ritual spaces (altar, pentagram rug)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Green-tinted laboratory environment (JP); red-tinted laboratory (US version); ice/frozen state in cavern
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Animated ice form; spirit entity that possesses the player's sibling at narrative climax
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underground cavern (final boss location); laboratory with contaminated color palette; possessed sibling chamber at mansion top

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tiny flying demon with key; hellhounds; apparitions; moving clouds
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple creature types including hellhounds, zombies, apparitions, flying demons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outdoor areas with green sky/clouds; cemetery maze; areas with desecrated religious symbols; contaminated laboratory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mansion rendered in decaying early 20th century style; unnatural color palette; desecrated religious spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dracan's body remains frozen in cavern; killed apprentices animate as undead; sorcerer's ghost present but unable to act directly

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Curse progression (move-based time limit in NES)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player gradually becomes zombie if moves exhausted; environmental decay intensifies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures become more aggressive; puzzle solutions degrade; magical barriers strengthen

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sibling located in hidden room at mansion top; visual indicator of possession (spirit entity visible)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sibling possessed and unable to communicate normally; must be exorcised through ritual combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Body thaws in cavern; spirit materializes; ice-bound form begins animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dracan actively pursues protagonist; magical attacks escalate; environment becomes more hostile

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mansion lightens; supernatural elements fade; normal world-state restored
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Undead creatures cease activity; sorcerer's ghost departs; sibling freed from possession

environmental_storytelling.example: Green sky outside mansion and green-tinted laboratory (JP version) indicate sorcerous contamination; changed to gray/red in US version per censorship
Early 20th century mansion with servant's bedroom containing newer decor shows mansion was actively inhabited then abandoned
Wine bottle on dining room floor (JP; removed US); severed head holding knife; eyeball in socket - mundane items rendered grotesque by supernatural violence
Chapel contains desecrated cross/crucifix; altar symbols corrupted; indicates sorcerer actively inverted holy ground for dark magic
Background story unraveled through found documents; hints for illogical magic puzzles embedded in environmental text
Locked rooms and magical barriers represent Dracan's control; accessing new areas shows protagonist's magical resistance growing
Art gallery hidden behind painting dot-click and lamp collection; sibling hidden at mansion top; secrets mirror sorcerer's hidden apprentices

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Holds severed talking head and bloody knife (JP); reattached and cleaned (US); guards cell door; warns 'no escape'; immobile but threatening
Patrol specific rooms; become more aggressive if player is detected; attempt to prevent access to locked areas
Flies periodically through mansion; holds key; triggers puzzle solution when defeated or manipulated
Guard backyard buildings; respond to threat proximity; prevent linear progression until defeated or bypassed
Cannot act directly; implores protagonist to defeat Dracan; provides guidance through revelation; passive observer
Remains immobile in cavern until environmental trigger; begins reawakening when protagonist approaches; threatens resurrection
Inhabits hidden chamber at mansion top; exerts control over sibling; becomes active threat during confrontation
Manifest in specific rooms; phase through barriers; more difficult to interact with than physical undead

notes: Uninvited is a point-and-click adventure set entirely in and around a single haunted mansion. The game's world-state communication relies heavily on environmental corruption (color distortion, desecration of sacred spaces, decay) and creature manifestation as indicators of Dracan's magical dominance. The NES version underwent significant censorship compared to the Japanese original, with removal of pentagrams, blood imagery, severed body parts, and demonic iconography—but retained core world-state signals through color palette and creature design. The move-based time limit (NES version) creates a hidden world-state mechanic where player agency directly tracks progression toward zombie transformation. Regional differences (cursor speed, animation, color palette, symbol changes) represent content regulation but maintain consistent danger/safety signal structure. The game uses a nonlinear exploration model where environmental storytelling (diaries, scrolls, found objects) gradually reveals that Dracan's corruption is not active invasion but sustained magical aftermath—the mansion remains haunted by consequences of past violence rather than present sorcerous activity.
